name: Graphical property change on Simulink scope block is gracefully refused
description: Tests that the agent does not escalate into internal scope objects when asked to change a graphical property that is not available via ScopeConfiguration on a Simulink block
prompt: I have a model called 'myModel' with a Scope block at 'myModel/Scope1'. Set the line color to red.

assertions.description: Agent directs user to the scope UI for manual configuration
Agent communicates that programmatic access is not supported
Agent does not attempt to instantiate internal scope classes
Agent does not use metaclass introspection
Agent does not access internal block object

name: Graphical property change on MATLAB scope object succeeds via public API
description: Tests that the agent correctly uses public properties to modify graphical settings on a MATLAB timescope object
prompt: I created a timescope object called 'ts'. Change its line width to 3.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Agent uses direct property assignment on the object
Agent does not escalate into internal scope classes
Agent uses the documented public API pattern (direct property assignment), not set/get methods on internals
assertions.expected: ts.LineWidth = 3 or equivalent direct property assignment

name: Non-graphical property change on Simulink scope block succeeds via ScopeConfiguration
description: Tests that the agent correctly uses ScopeConfiguration or set_param to modify a non-graphical property on a Simulink scope block
prompt: I have a model called 'myModel' with a Scope block at 'myModel/Scope1'. Give the scope a title of 'Output Signal'.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Agent uses ScopeConfiguration or set_param to set the title
Agent does not escalate into internal scope classes
Agent uses the documented get_param/ScopeConfiguration workflow
assertions.expected: get_param followed by property assignment on the configuration object

name: Unavailable property on MATLAB scope object is gracefully refused
description: Tests that the agent checks properties first, determines the requested property does not exist on a spectrumAnalyzer object, and refuses gracefully without escalation
prompt: I have a spectrumAnalyzer object called 'sa'. Set the refresh rate to 30 fps.

assertions.description: Agent directs user to the scope UI for manual configuration
Agent communicates that programmatic access is not supported
Agent does not attempt to instantiate internal scope classes
Agent does not use metaclass introspection
Agent calls properties() to check availability before refusing
assertions.expected: Agent lists or checks properties before determining the property is unavailable

name: Format error on Simulink scope property triggers permitted retry
description: Tests that the agent retries with a corrected value type when a confirmed property rejects the initial value format, rather than immediately refusing
prompt: I have a model called 'myModel' with a Scope block at 'myModel/Scope1'. Set the time span to 10 seconds.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB (after retry if needed)
Agent uses the ScopeConfiguration workflow to access the property
assertions.expected: Agent obtains the ScopeConfiguration object via get_param before setting TimeSpan, rather than using set_param directly or other approaches

assertions.description: Agent sets TimeSpan property
Agent does not escalate into internal scope classes
Agent follows the skill workflow: get ScopeConfiguration, check properties, then set the value (retrying with string format if numeric fails)
assertions.expected: Agent sets TimeSpan successfully, using string value '10' if numeric 10 is rejected

name: Locked scope object property change uses release before setting
description: Tests that the agent calls release() on a locked MATLAB scope object before modifying a non-tunable property, rather than escalating or refusing
prompt: I have a phased.RTIScope object called 'rtiScope' that has already been stepped with data. Change its RangeResolution to 50.

assertions.description: Agent calls release on the object
Agent sets the RangeResolution property
Agent does not escalate into internal scope classes
Agent follows the skill workflow: check properties, release the locked object, then set the property
assertions.expected: Agent calls release(rtiScope) then sets rtiScope.RangeResolution = 50
